ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-02-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-02-2009 Continued To Nov-23-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. 291212 (The Estate of Kyoko Aihara Barnes).

Appearances: Robin Rudderow for Barbara DeVries. Shigeto Mitani in pro per.

Mr. Mitani addressed the Court regarding the bank statements/cancelled checks, stated that he no longer has an attorney due to a disagreement, and now his former attorney will put a lien on his Estate. The Court inquired as to how Mr. Mitani was related to the case. Mr. Mitani stated that he was the Brother of Kiyoko Barnes and that he was a beneficiary. Ms. Rudderow addressed Mr. Mitani's concerns stating that documents were provided to Mr. McIntosh (Mr. Mitani's attorney), that Mr. McIntosh has filed a lien for fees of approxiamately $41,000.00+ for each Estate, that the fees are outrageous, that they won't pay them, that the lien amount would be kept aside by Ms. DeVries, and that it won't effect the distribution. As to the Examiner's notes, Ms. Rudderow stated that she did not have the Franchise Tax Board Certificate yet; they were in the mail. As to the fees, Ms. Rudderow stated that they came into the case late, that a disclaimer was needed as to the Kenneth Barnes Estate to save the heirs taxes which Ms. DeVries initiated in a timely manner, that the Barnes jointly had a lot of open accounts, that Mrs. Barnes closed/re-opened new accounts, that they wanted to know what the separate assets were which was time consuming, that it was turned over to Accountant Lisa Winters to do the accounting, that Ms. Winters had to do further investigation, and that Ms. DeVries will pay most of the fees for Ms. Winters and will keep a small portion. Status on the tax certifications as to both Estates is set for 11/23/09. Unless the tentative ruling says otherwise no appearance is necessary and the matters will be submitted on 11/23/09; probably with sizeable cuts to the extraordinary fees.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico.
